# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kubernetes Deployment manifests with Istio sidecar injection disabled using sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false" annotation. Skip sidecar proxies for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies. This is for services that require low latency without proxy overhead.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: v1  # yaml version of the API (in this case, Version one)  
kind: Deployment    # kind of object you're creating in Kubernetes; ie., a resource to create or replace instances of an application. The Kind will determine which container image is used for that deployment    
metadata:  # information about your deployed objects - usually required info like name and namespace  
  labels:      # additional metadata associated with the object, often stored in tags format   
    app: myapp        # unique label key/value pairs. You can use this to organize them based on application or other needs (ie., different namespaces)    
  annotations:          # user-defined information about your objects - these provide more detailed description and control of how Kubernetes should manage the object's life cycle  
    sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false"      # specify if we want to inject istio sidecars or not, set it as "true". This can be useful for debugging purpose but often a user will just leave this annotation empty in production deployments    
spec:       # details of the object - what you are creating (ie., define how your pod should behave)  
  replicas:     3         # specify that we want to create three instances   
  selector:            # What stateful set is created when it's running a Pod? If any new containers start, they get added into thesePod. A unique combination of labels (in this case "app": myApp) must match the selectors in order for them to join  
     matchLabels:      # The application being run by each pod is defined here   
       app: myapp          # specific label key/value pairs that define what kind of Pod should be created. If any are matched, thosePod will include this new container into it'  'replicas':  3               '# number and type (cpu or memory) constraints for the deployment - these restrictions apply to every member in a StatefulSet by default
       tier: frontend      # additional label key/value pairs that define which physical topology(s)/network configuration should be used. Also, each Pod can have multiple containers with different labels set due   'replicaCount': 3               '# number of replicas to keep alive in the deployment - if not specified then it will default as a single instance
       service: enabled     # Specifies whether or not endpoints are injected into pods and managed by Istio. Set this option true for specific services that need direct network access without mesh policies (like some API gateways). It's often used in production deployments   'serviceAccountName': my-app    '# the name of Service account to use, can be a secret key or serviceaccount from an existing Project at Auth0. Required if not specified when creating pod security policy
